Adaptability.effect: STAB becomes x2 instead of x1.5 — Basculegion's Wave Crash and a charged Last Respects hit absurdly hard, even more so in rain.

Aerilate.effect: Normal-type moves become Flying-type with a power boost — Salamence-Mega's Double-Edge becomes a boosted Flying attack.

Armor Tail.effect: Blocks priority moves aimed at the holder or its ally (Fake Out, Sucker Punch, Extreme Speed, Prankster status). Does NOT block normal-bracket moves or sleep — a slow Taunt still shuts Farigiraf down.

Chlorophyll.effect: Doubles Speed in sun (Venusaur, Scovillain-Mega) — the speed engine of sun teams; useless once the weather is stolen.

Clear Body.effect: Opposing effects cannot lower its stats — Dragapult ignores Intimidate and Snarl/Icy Wind drops.

Competitive.effect: +2 SpA whenever an opponent lowers its stats — Intimidate (or Parting Shot) against Milotic is a gift. Never cycle Intimidate into it.

Compound Eyes.effect: Boosts accuracy ~30% — Vivillon's Sleep Powder lands at 97%.

Cursed Body.effect: May disable a move that hits the holder. Gengar and Froslass run this only pre-Mega — it is replaced (Shadow Tag / Snow Warning) once they Mega Evolve.

Defiant.effect: +2 Atk whenever an opponent lowers its stats — Kingambit turns Intimidate and Parting Shot into a free snowball. The common Champions tournament ability on Kingambit (Supreme Overlord seen on some).

Disguise.effect: Mimikyu: the first hit it takes is nullified (the disguise busts instead of taking damage).

Drizzle.effect: Sets rain on entry for 5 turns (Pelipper, Politoed): Water +50%, Fire -50%, Hurricane/Thunder 100%, Electro Shot instant. Weather wars: the LAST setter to enter wins — re-switching the setter re-sets the rain.

Drought.effect: Sets sun on entry for 5 turns (Charizard-Mega-Y, Torkoal): Fire +50%, Water -50%, instant Solar Beam. It boosts EVERYONE's Fire, enemy included; the last weather setter to enter wins.

Flower Veil.effect: Protects Grass-type allies (Floette-Mega's ability) from stat drops and status conditions.

Friend Guard.effect: Reduces damage taken by the ally (Maushold) — stacks with its Follow Me/Helping Hand support kit.

Gale Wings.effect: At full HP the holder's Flying moves gain priority — Talonflame fires Brave Bird and Tailwind first; stops working once it is chipped (recoil included).

Good as Gold.effect: Immune to status moves aimed at the holder — Taunt, Spore, Will-O-Wisp and Perish Song all fail against it.

Grassy Surge.effect: Sets Grassy Terrain on entry (Rillaboom) — enables priority Grassy Glide for grounded users.

Guts.effect: +50% Attack when statused — Ursaluna WANTS the burn/poison and Facade doubles on top of it.

Hospitality.effect: Heals its ally a bit on entry (Sinistcha) — passive value on every redirect pivot.

Huge Power.effect: Doubles Attack — Azumarill and Mawile-Mega hit monstrously despite low base stats; Mawile compensates the low Speed with Sucker Punch.

Illusion.effect: Zoroark enters disguised as the LAST mon in the team; the illusion breaks at the first damage taken — spread moves unmask it for free.

Inner Focus.effect: Cannot flinch (Dragonite pre-Mega) — immune to Fake Out's flinch on the turn before Mega Evolving.

Intimidate.effect: -1 Atk to both opponents on EVERY entry — Incineroar cycles it with Parting Shot for compounding drops. Backfires hard into Defiant (Kingambit +2 Atk) and Competitive (Milotic +2 SpA); Clear Body ignores it.

Levitate.effect: Immune to Ground moves — it is why Rotom-Wash has no x4 weakness and only fears Grass.

Magic Bounce.effect: REFLECTS status moves back at the user — Taunt, Spore, Will-O-Wisp and even Perish Song bounce off Hatterene, making her the safest Trick Room setter.

Magic Guard.effect: Takes no indirect damage (hazards, weather, Leech Seed, recoil-style chip) — but direct attacks still hurt Clefable, and Perish Song still KOs through it.

Magnet Pull.effect: Traps opposing Steel-types — Kingambit, Tinkaton and Aegislash cannot switch out while Magnezone is in.

Mega Launcher.effect: Boosts pulse/aura moves (Blastoise-Mega: Dark Pulse, Dragon Pulse, Aura Sphere).

Mold Breaker.effect: The holder's moves ignore the target's ability (Tinkaton) — hits through Levitate, Multiscale and friends.

Multiscale.effect: Halves damage taken at full HP — Dragonite-Mega survives one big hit from full; chip it or use spread/multi-hit moves to break the scale first.

Overcoat.effect: Immune to powder moves (Spore, Sleep Powder, Rage Powder) and weather chip — same powder immunity Grass-types get for free.

Parental Bond.effect: Every move hits twice, the second hit weaker (Kangaskhan-Mega) — double hits break Focus Sash, Multiscale and Disguise.

Pixilate.effect: Normal moves become Fairy-type with a power boost — Sylveon's Hyper Voice becomes a spread Fairy nuke that also ignores redirection (sound + spread). Soundproof still blocks it.

Prankster.effect: Status moves gain +1 priority (Whimsicott Tailwind/Encore, Sableye Taunt/Quash/screens, Grimmsnarl, Klefki). Blocked by Armor Tail and Psychic Terrain; Prankster-boosted status fails against Dark-types.

Propeller Tail.effect: Ignores redirection — Barraskewda hits the chosen target straight through Follow Me/Rage Powder.

Protean.effect: Changes the user's type to the move it is about to use (Meowscarada) — every attack gets STAB.

Psychic Surge.effect: Sets Psychic Terrain on entry (Indeedee): blocks ALL priority moves against grounded targets (Fake Out, Sucker Punch, Jet Punch, Extreme Speed, Prankster) and boosts Psychic moves.

Regenerator.effect: Heals about a third of max HP on switch-out — Amoonguss keeps coming back to redirect again.

Rough Skin.effect: Contact attackers take chip damage (Garchomp) — punishes Fake Out, U-turn and multi-hit contact moves.

Sand Rush.effect: Doubles Speed in sandstorm (Excadrill, partnered with Tyranitar-Mega/Hippowdon); mid-slow outside the sand.

Sand Stream.effect: Sets sandstorm on entry (Tyranitar-Mega, Hippowdon): chip damage + Sand Rush enabler + Rock SpD boost. Weather wars: the last setter to enter wins.

Scrappy.effect: The holder's Normal and Fighting moves HIT Ghost-types — Lopunny-Mega's Fake Out and High Jump Kick connect on Gengar/Basculegion/Sinistcha.

Shadow Tag.effect: Opponents cannot switch out — Gengar-Mega's Perish Trap engine (active only AFTER Mega Evolving; pre-Mega it is Cursed Body). Ghost-types are immune, and pivot moves (U-turn/Volt Switch/Flip Turn/Parting Shot/Teleport) still escape.

Snow Warning.effect: Sets snow on entry (Froslass-Mega once evolved, Ninetales-Alola): enables Aurora Veil, +50% Def to Ice-types, Blizzard 100%. Last weather setter wins.

Soundproof.effect: Immune to sound moves INCLUDING allied ones — Kommo-o ignores its own team's Perish Song and stays on the field as the trap win-condition; also blanks Hyper Voice and Snarl.

Speed Boost.effect: +1 Speed at the end of every turn (Espathra) — Baton Pass ships the accumulated boosts to a teammate; fragile to Taunt and phazing.

Spicy Spray.effect: When the Pokemon takes damage from a move, it burns the attacker.

Stalwart.effect: Ignores redirection — Archaludon and Mega Skarmory (custom Champions Mega ability) always hit the chosen target straight through Follow Me/Rage Powder/Sinistcha.

Stamina.effect: +1 Defense every time it is hit — Archaludon becomes a growing wall and its Body Press scales with every hit taken. KO it within 2 turns or it snowballs.

Stance Change.effect: Aegislash stays in tanky Shield form until it attacks (then flips to frail, hard-hitting Blade form); King's Shield flips it back to Shield.

Supreme Overlord.effect: Attack boosted for each fainted ally (source claims ~+20% per KO; mainline is +10% Atk/SpA — unverified in Champions) — Kingambit is brought as the LAST of the 4; fast-KO plans (Perish included) charge it for the opponent.

Swift Swim.effect: Doubles Speed in rain (Barraskewda) — near dead weight without the weather.

Thermal Exchange.effect: Fire moves feed Baxcalibur +1 Attack instead of meaningful damage, and it cannot be burned — the rising anti-sun tech; hitting it with Fire is worse than useless.

Toxic Debris.effect: Scatters Toxic Spikes on the attacker's side when hit by physical moves (Glimmora's hazard-lead engine; its Mega ability is a custom Champions variant).

Trace.effect: Copies an opponent's ability on entry (Gardevoir).

Unaware.effect: Ignores the opponent's stat changes when taking and dealing damage — setup sweepers cannot out-boost Dondozo/Clefable. Note: Perish Song ignores Unaware right back.

Unburden.effect: Speed DOUBLES when the held item is consumed and stays doubled while the holder remains in — Sneasler's autocombo: Close Combat drops defenses, White Herb restores them and is consumed, Unburden fires. Paralysis ruins the whole plan; the boost is lost on switch-out.

Berserk.effect: Raises SpA when the holder drops below half HP; Drampa can turn a survived hit into stronger Dragon Pulse/Hyper Voice pressure.

Solid Rock.effect: Reduces super-effective damage taken; Camerupt-Mega still hates Water ×4, but marginal hits are less clean.

Frisk.effect: Reveals held items on entry; Gourgeist uses it as scouting value before setting Trick Room.

Water Bubble.effect: Doubles Water move power and prevents burn; Araquanid turns Liquidation into a serious Trick Room nuke.

Rock Head.effect: Prevents recoil from recoil moves; Hisuian Arcanine can click Flare Blitz without self-chip.

Electric Surge.effect: Sets Electric Terrain on entry; Mega Raichu X brings terrain control and boosts grounded Electric attacks.

No Guard.effect: All moves used by and against the holder hit; Mega Raichu Y turns low-accuracy attacks such as Zap Cannon and Focus Blast into reliable pressure, but also makes incoming attacks unavoidable.

Zero to Hero.effect: Palafin transforms into Hero form (160 Atk) after switching out once — Encore/Taunt the switch cycle to deny or punish the flip.

Mirror Armor.effect: Reflects stat drops back to the user; Intimidate and Icy Wind-style debuffs can backfire into Corviknight teams.

Technician.effect: Boosts moves with 60 BP or less by 50%; Scizor's Bullet Punch becomes real priority damage.

Queenly Majesty.effect: Blocks opposing priority moves that target the user or its ally.

Solar Power.effect: Boosts Special Attack in sun but chips HP each turn while sun is active.

Torrent.effect: Boosts Water moves when HP is low.

Sharpness.effect: Boosts slicing moves such as Psycho Cut and Sacred Sword.

Flash Fire.effect: Immune to Fire moves and powers up the user's Fire moves after absorbing one.

Flame Body.effect: Contact moves against this Pokemon can burn the attacker.

Blaze.effect: Boosts Fire moves when HP is low.

Dry Skin.effect: Heals from Water, is hurt by Fire and sun, and recovers in rain.

Sturdy.effect: Survives a hit from full HP with 1 HP in mainline mechanics.

Lightning Rod.effect: Redirects single-target Electric moves and raises SpA when hit by Electric.

Static.effect: Contact moves against this Pokemon can paralyze the attacker.

Pressure.effect: Increases opposing PP usage in mainline play; no direct matchup flag in the current engine.
